examples.title: Pseudocode: Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering
examples.description: This pseudocode demonstrates the agglomerative approach to hierarchical clustering.
examples.explanation: This pseudocode outlines the steps involved in agglomerative hierarchical clustering. It starts by initializing each data point as its own cluster, then iteratively merges the closest clusters until only one cluster remains. The distance matrix is updated after each merge, and the dendrogram is constructed from the final cluster hierarchy.

examples.title: Python: Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering
examples.description: This Python code implements the agglomerative approach to hierarchical clustering using the Scikit-learn library.
examples.code:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.cluster import AgglomerativeClustering
from sklearn.metrics import pairwise_distances

# Define the dataset
X = np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4], [5, 6], [7, 8]])

# Define the distance metric and linkage method
metric = 'euclidean'
linkage = 'ward'

# Compute the initial distance matrix
D = pairwise_distances(X, metric=metric)

# Initialize the AgglomerativeClustering object
ac = AgglomerativeClustering(n_clusters=2, affinity=metric, linkage=linkage)

# Fit the model to the data
ac.fit(X)

# Get the cluster labels
labels = ac.labels_

# Print the cluster labels
print(labels)
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates the use of the Scikit-learn library to perform agglomerative hierarchical clustering. It defines a dataset, computes the initial distance matrix, initializes the AgglomerativeClustering object, fits the model to the data, and prints the cluster labels.

examples.title: C: Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering
examples.description: This C code implements the agglomerative approach to hierarchical clustering.
examples.explanation: This C code implements the agglomerative approach to hierarchical clustering. It defines a structure for a cluster, computes the initial distance matrix, merges the closest clusters until only one cluster remains, and updates the distance matrix after each merge.
